Chord Progression Spicer

A generator that spices up chord progressions. Made with OpenAI.

Output: letters & keys

Generated progression

Playable loop

Each chord plays for 1 bar in 4/4.

Guitar tab (EADGBe)

Export

What the spice options mean

Secondary dominants
Adds a quick dominant 7 chord before another chord, like giving that next chord its own mini V-I pull. To a basic musician: it is a short setup chord that makes the next chord feel more expected and satisfying.
Modal interchange
Borrowing a chord from a closely related scale or mode, often making the progression sound darker, brighter, or more surprising. To a basic musician: it is like briefly using a chord from a parallel musical color, then coming back home.
Extensions (7/9)
Adds extra notes on top of the basic triad, such as a 7th or 9th. To a basic musician: the chord root stays the same, but the sound becomes fuller, smoother, or more jazzy.
ii-V / Tritone subs
Replaces or leads into dominant chords with classic jazz movement. A ii-V creates a strong path toward the next chord; a tritone substitution swaps a dominant chord for another dominant chord three whole steps away for a smoother, bluesier twist.
